reviews.review_msg: Great and passionate professor, but I often felt like I did pretty well on a test only to get 75%. He seemed pleased with a class average of 75% though. Lots of long readings but it's not the end of the world if you don't do them all. His slides are very uninformative though. You have to take notes at a very rapid pace to get all the information.

Sedra is the best professor I have ever had. He has such passion for the subjects he teaches and answers all questions with kindness and enthusiasm. If you haven't taken any Middle Eastern history classes, I strongly suggest taking 151 and 355, as I have walked away from both courses with a deep appreciation for the subjects.
